item.title: Iran war sends gas prices climbing — what happens next?
item.description: An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.

item.title: Choosing Khamenei’s successor
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Iran moderating.

item.title: Markwayne Mullin’s opportunity to reset immigration policy
item.description: Kristi Noem’s ouster at DHS allows for a needed overhaul.

item.title: In defense of sugar
item.description: Republicans used to be annoyed at government telling people what to eat.

item.title: The dangerous rise of decapitation warfare
item.description: Removing an enemy leader is easy compared with producing a stable political outcome.

item.title: Why it matters that the government is messing with health databases
item.description: There is no replacement for reliable data from federal government databases.

item.title: Kristi Noem was a nightmare for immigration hawks
item.description: Sloppy enforcement and tone-deaf messaging have undermined Homeland Security’s credibility.

item.title: The absurdity of imprisoning parents for their children’s crimes
item.description: The guilty verdict against Colin Gray in school shooting case creates a dangerous precedent.

item.title: Scammers are getting smarter. We must, too.
item.description: AI scams, data center NIMBYsim, SNAP and access, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: With Iran, international law has lost its credibility
item.description: International lawyers defend a framework that cannot capture real moral differences in war.

item.title: The Supreme Court makes a common-sense decision on parental rights in California
item.description: The government of California doesn’t supersede parental rights. Even at school.

item.title: America’s dating crisis is getting worse. What went wrong?
item.description: Young people are struggling to find love. It’s time to bring back the matchmaker.

item.title: A violent economy is taking root in Venezuela — and could undermine Trump
item.description: Criminal mining in southern Venezuela hinders U.S. attempts to secure supply chains.

item.title: Anthropic takes on Hegseth and Trump
item.description: The president and his team have gone too far — again — in trying to bend a private business to their will.

item.title: The NBA’s covid guy deserves a break
item.description: What Rudy Gobert’s unfortunate microphone moment was really about.

item.title: The best argument for letting America go? A polymath nailed it.
item.description: Adam Smith’s “The Wealth of Nations” remains as relevant 250 years later.

item.title: An unlikely AI optimist
item.description: The European Central Bank says adoption of the technology creates more jobs than it eliminates.

item.title: It would be easier to fund DHS with better leadership
item.description: A lack of transparency and wasteful spending undermine the case to end the department’s shutdown.

item.title: Don’t gamble with airport security
item.description: Readers on TSA reform, stock trading in Congress and more.

item.title: Mississippi shouldn’t prefer student athletes over students
item.description: Football recruitment isn’t a good reason to treat NIL money differently than other income.

item.title: What Texas will discover about James Talarico
item.description: Texas Republicans gave the Democrat an opening. His refusal to moderate on issues might close it.

item.title: Cartoon by Joe Heller
item.description: Joe Heller cartoon on daylight saving time.

item.title: The Democratic establishment gets the night it wanted
item.description: Good nights in Texas and North Carolina offer relief — but the progressive challenge is far from over.

item.title: Putin is failing. These charts prove it.
item.description: Data from the battlefield show Ukraine is holding its own.

item.title: I was a Gen X ‘man cave’ dad. Then I learned better.
item.description: Fathers know best when the door to the dad retreat is unlocked.

item.title: These bike laws are tyranny on wheels
item.description: Policymakers are making a basic form of transportation and a familiar element of childhood less accessible.

item.title: Why U.S. military officers need to go to Harvard. And Columbia. And …
item.description: The civil-military-academic ecosystem is the foundation of American geopolitical power.

item.title: How to defeat a dangerous ideological drift in the Middle East
item.description: Against Iran, the United Arab Emirates, Israel and Azerbaijan should join forces.

item.title: What comes next in Iran
item.description: On Feb. 28, the U.S. and Israel initiated strikes on Iran. What comes next? Host Megan McArdle sits down with Washington Post Columnist David Ignatius to discuss Iran's response, whether the U.S. can sustain a prolonged conflict, what the war signals to adversaries like China and the economic risks of a closed Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Maryland’s electric school bus debacle
item.description: Unrealistic mandates drove an unworkable plan in Montgomery County.
